name: fe1_fresh_right_flagged_left
drl:
rule TJ1 salience 5 when $a : E0() from entry-point "S1" $b : E2($t : tag, this before[0ms,50ms] $a) then insertLogical(new D($t)); end
rule RN salience 9 when not D() P() then end
rule K salience 7 when $b : E2(ts == 0) P() then delete($b); end
rule R6 salience 6 when P() then end
rule RL salience 1 when P() then end
_finding: FLAG-EAGER PAIR ARC (own arc, split from the D-177 halt work per Bryan): the ENGINE pairs a fresh right-insert P(1) with the flag-expired left-memory partner E2(0) at a plain join inside a STREAM session — the oracle refuses the pair (D-102 mechanism 4 flag-eager: a pending-expired event makes NO NEW join pairs). Witness: K@7 `E2(ts==0) P()` at clock 101 (E2(0) deadline 101, expired-pending): oracle 3x [TJ1, R6, TJ1, RL] (K never activates); engine [TJ1, K, R6, TJ1, RL] (K fires). Mechanism UNCONFIRMED: flag-walk gap on the right-insert->left-memory scan vs ==boundary arithmetic on this path. Non-TMS, no executor involvement — needs its own minimal cell + bisect. Arc file: ~/.claude/plans/flag-eager-pair-arc.md.
